summary: 11 concrete items surfaced from 23 qualifying stories; strongest signals came from sandbox tools, retro-video tools, medical AI, and weird-interface experiments.

items.name: Zeroserve
items.source_story_title: Zeroserve: A zero-config web server you can script with eBPF
items.summary: Zero-config eBPF web server that serves sites from a tarball.
items.why_included: HN treated it as a real nginx/Caddy alternative, not just a demo.
items.evidence: A commenter said new servers need benchmarks to beat techempower, and another said nginx/Caddy still define the bar.

items.name: Judge0
items.source_story_title: Running Python code in a sandbox with MicroPython and WASM
items.summary: Open-source sandboxed code execution system for humans and AI.
items.why_included: Commenters explicitly named it as the sandbox to reach for first.
items.evidence: A commenter suggested Judge0 for sandboxing instead of reinventing it for LLMs.

items.name: BrowserPod
items.source_story_title: Running Python code in a sandbox with MicroPython and WASM
items.summary: Browser-based WebAssembly sandboxes for secure local code execution.
items.why_included: It was the browser-local sandbox option with a different deployment model.
items.evidence: A commenter described BrowserPod as the browser-based sandbox path, with Node now and Python/Rust coming.

items.name: Monty
items.source_story_title: Running Python code in a sandbox with MicroPython and WASM
items.summary: Rust Python subset/runtime for fast, sandboxed LLM code.
items.why_included: It stood out as the ultra-fast Rust sandboxed Python option.
items.evidence: The thread highlighted Monty as a tiny, isolated interpreter for LLM-generated code.

items.name: Ntsc-rs
items.source_story_title: Ntsc-rs – open-source video emulation of analog TV and VHS artifacts
items.summary: Open-source analog TV and VHS artifact emulator for retro video.
items.why_included: HN found the retro-video look convincing and worth trying.
items.evidence: Readers asked for rarer analog artifacts and praised how convincing the output looked.

items.name: HackTV
items.source_story_title: Ntsc-rs – open-source video emulation of analog TV and VHS artifacts
items.summary: Analog TV transmitter for HackRF and SDR signal experiments.
items.why_included: It was the concrete analog-TV tool the retro-video thread kept pointing back to.
items.evidence: The thread named HackTV as the analog-transmitter tool already doing the signal work.

items.name: Pokemon Emerald WASM port
items.source_story_title: Pokemon Emerald Ported to WebAssembly (100k FPS)
items.summary: Pokémon Emerald recompiled to run in WebAssembly.
items.why_included: The browser port drew enthusiasm plus practical bugfix and save-state talk.
items.evidence: Comments discussed save support, controls, PWA support, and bug fixes rather than dismissing the project.

items.name: Poincake
items.source_story_title: Show HN: Infinite canvas notes in the non-Euclidean Poincaré disk
items.summary: Infinite-canvas note-taking app in hyperbolic space.
items.why_included: Commenters found the hyperbolic note UI intuitive enough to want on a tablet.
items.evidence: People called it “weirdly intuitive” and said it would be “fantastic on a tablet”.

items.name: HyperRogue
items.source_story_title: Show HN: Infinite canvas notes in the non-Euclidean Poincaré disk
items.why_included: It was cited as the closest existing example of the same geometry idea.
items.evidence: A commenter explicitly said to look at HyperRogue because it already uses the same model.

items.name: Splash
items.summary: Custom colour format designed to reduce choice paralysis.
items.why_included: The thread surfaced a real design question: whether a new color notation helps.
items.evidence: Commenters debated hex, RGB, HSV, HCL, LAB, and palette browsing as alternatives.

items.name: OpenEvidence
items.source_story_title: Police in England and Wales told to halt AI use in court statements
items.summary: Medical AI copilot for clinicians with evidence-first answers.
items.why_included: The thread held it up as an evidence-first alternative to freeform LLM drafting.
items.evidence: A commenter pointed to OpenEvidence as the system that links users to relevant papers instead of answering directly.
